"https://api.github.com/users/Amoifr", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-55877", "cvss": {"score": 6.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-79", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')"}], "description": "### Description

The `ux_icon()` Twig function is marked `is_safe=['html']`, so Twig never escapes its output. `Icon::toHtml()` inlines the SVG source verbatim into the page. Browsers execute `<script>` elements and `on*` event-handler attributes found inside inline SVG, making any unsanitized icon a vector for cross-site scripting.

Two code paths were affected. In the local file path, `Icon::fromFile()` only stripped `<script>` elements that were direct children of `<svg>`, leaving nested scripts and all `on*` attributes untouched despite a code comment claiming broader protection. In the Iconify on-demand path (enabled by default), the remote JSON `body` field was wrapped into an `Icon` object with no sanitization at all. Concrete attack vectors include a malicious SVG icon pack from a third-party theme or downloaded icon set, or a controlled Iconify endpoint configured via `iconify.endpoint` (including a poisoned cache).

### Resolution

Introducing an `IconFactory` that centralizes sanitization across every icon source before an `Icon` object is created. The sanitizer removes script-capable elements (`script`, `foreignObject`, `iframe`, `object`, `embed`), SMIL animations targeting `on*`, `href`, or `xlink:href` attributes, CDATA sections, processing instructions, all `on*` attributes, and `javascript:`, `vbscript:`, and `data:text/html` URL schemes. 
`<style>` elements are kept for theming but have any handlers stripped. Icons that contain none of these constructs are byte-for-byte identical after sanitization. 

### Credits

Symfony would like to thank Pascal Cescon for reporting the issue and Hugo Alliaume for providing the fix.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00194, "percentile": 0.09253}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6v8j-33hc-mv84", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-19T21:42:15Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6v8j-33hc-mv84", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6v8j-33hc-mv84"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-55877"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-19T21:42:15Z", "references": ["https://github.com/symfony/ux/security/advisories/GHSA-6v8j-33hc-mv84", "https://github.com/FriendsOfPHP/security-advisories/blob/master/symfony/ux-icons/CVE-2026-55877.yaml", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6v8j-33hc-mv84"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/symfony/ux/security-advisories/GHSA-6v8j-33hc-mv84", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/symfony/ux", "summary": "symfony/ux-icons: XSS via unsanitized SVG content in local files and Iconify on-demand responses", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-19T21:42:15Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6v8j-33hc-mv84", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.36.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "symfony/ux-icons"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.17.0, < 2.36.1"}, {"first_patched_version": "3.2.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "symfony/ux-icons"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.0.0, < 3.2.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: symfony/ux-icons: XSS via unsanitized SVG content in local files and Iconify on-demand responses
